headline: Why ServiceNow (NOW) Stock Is Nosediving
summary: Shares of enterprise workflow automation company ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) fell 6.7% in the morning session after reports of a ceasefire breach in the Middle East spiked market volatility as fears grew that a fragile U.S.-Iran truce would unravel.

content.title: Why ServiceNow (NOW) Stock Is Nosediving
content.summary: Shares of enterprise workflow automation company ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) fell 6.7% in the morning session after reports of a ceasefire breach in the Middle East spiked market volatility as fears grew that a fragile U.S.-Iran truce would unravel.

headline: UBS downgrades its only Buy-rated application software stock amid AI risks
summary: Investing.com -- UBS downgraded ServiceNow to neutral from buy in a note on Friday, cutting its price target to $100 from $170, as the bank reassesses whether the enterprise software company's competitive position is as resilient to artificial intelligence disruption as previously believed.

headline: UBS Cuts ServiceNow to Neutral With a $100 Target: Yesterday’s Selloff Was Just the Beginning?
summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) stock dropped 7.86% on Thursday, April 9, and continued lower Friday morning as a broader software sector selloff accelerated on fears that managed AI agents could disrupt enterprise software incumbents. Now UBS is piling on, cutting ServiceNow from Buy to Neutral with a fresh $100 price target — and the stock is already ... headline: Palantir Is Down 17% in Three Days: Inside the Selloff That Has the AI Platform King Reeling

headline: The “SaaS-Pocalypse” Continues: Cloudflare, ServiceNow, CrowdStrike Under Fire as Anthropic Rewrites the Rules
summary: The so-called “SaaS-pocalypse” is back, and it’s hitting harder today than it did yesterday. Anthropic’s release of “Claude Code Security,” an AI-driven security product, has rattled investors across the enterprise software and cybersecurity space, sending shares of Cloudflare (NYSE:NET), ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW), and CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) sharply lower in Friday morning trading. headline: ServiceNow Gets a Downgrade. AI Worries Sink Another Software Stock.
summary: Like so many other software stocks,  ServiceNow  has plummeted to start 2026.  Analysts at the investment bank downgraded  ServiceNow  to Neutral from Buy and slashed their price target to $100 from $170 in a research note.  The workflow automation company isn’t getting replaced by artificial intelligence, UBS said, but enterprises aren’t opening their wallets for non-AI tools either.

content.title: ServiceNow Gets a Downgrade. AI Worries Sink Another Software Stock.
content.summary: Like so many other software stocks,  ServiceNow  has plummeted to start 2026.  Analysts at the investment bank downgraded  ServiceNow  to Neutral from Buy and slashed their price target to $100 from $170 in a research note.  The workflow automation company isn’t getting replaced by artificial intelligence, UBS said, but enterprises aren’t opening their wallets for non-AI tools either.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Lumentum, Coherent,  Palo Alto, TSMC, Organon, Palantir, ServiceNow, and More
summary: Stocks were mixed Friday after U.S. inflation data showed consumer price inflation rose to its highest level in nearly two years in the wake of the Iran war.  The stocks were rising after Lumentum CEO Michael Hurston cited strong AI-related demand.  J.P. Morgan analysts also raised their price targets on shares of both optical equipment companies.
